text:
Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

Sports fans have already enjoyed a packed summer, but even though Euro 2024 and Wimbledon are over, there’s plenty more sporting action on the way. On Friday (July 26), the 2024Olympic Gameswill kick off in Paris, with more than 300 events in 32 sports taking place over two weeks.

The games will run until August 11 and feature all the classic events including athletics, triathlon and diving, as well as recent additions like skateboarding and sport climbing. Paris will see a new addition added to the Olympic schedule in the form of breaking – or breakdancing – which will make its debut at La Concorde on August 9 as competitors face off in a series of solo battles.

However, unlike previous years, most of the events will not be broadcast live on BBC. That’s because in 2018, Discovery took over the exclusive rights for the games and is set to stream all 32 sports on its platform.

READ MORE:Hyperoptic takes on Sky and Virgin with £12 a month broadband deal

READ MORE:Virgin Media takes aim at Sky with 'lowest ever' TV and broadband prices



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

The streamer – which is available viaAmazon’s Prime Video– will air live and rolling coverage of each event, starting on Wednesday (July 24) at 2pm with Argentina v Morocco and Uzbekistan v Spain in the men’s football.Discovery+is scheduled to broadcast a whopping 3,800 hours of sport from the Olympics, compared to BBC’s 250 hours.

This includes this year’s opening ceremony, which marks the first ever to take place outside a stadium. Kicking off on Friday at 6.30pm BST, the spectacle will be held in the heart of the French capital along the river Seine.

In what organisers have promised will be ‘ambitious, historic [and] spectacular’, the ceremony will see the parade of athletes held on the river with boats for each national delegation. After this, viewers can catch all the action in the swimming, cycling, gymnastics, tennis and much more across two weeks of sport.

Don't miss:This Works anti-ageing cream 'smooths lines on neck' after one use say shoppers

Don't miss:'Phenomenal' Oral-B electric toothbrush has £270 price drop on Amazon

Don't miss:Keep an eye on your home with rare Amazon security deal

The Paris 2024 Olympics will be broadcast onDiscovery+, which is available withAmazon Prime’s Discovery+ channeland currently includes an Olympic Games special offer. New and returning Discovery+ subscribers can sign up for the streamer’s Standard monthly plan and pay£3.99 a monthuntil the end of 2024 compared to the usual £6.99.

This also includes access to Eurosport 1 and 2, Discovery’s packed on-demand catalogue, plus live channels like Animal Planet, Discovery History, DMAX and TLC. This limited-time offer is available on theDiscovery+ Standardplan until August 11 via Amazon’s Prime Video channels.

On members’ first billing date on or after January 1, 2025, the rate will revert to the then-current monthly subscription rate – currently £6.99 – until cancelled.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Discovery isn’t the only brand to have dropped its prices for sports fans, as Sky has slashed the price of itsSky Stream, Sky Sports, Sky TV and Netflixbundle by £96. For £42 a month, members can enjoy round the clock coverage across nine dedicated sports channels alongside premium channels like Sky Atlantic and Sky Showcase.

title: Where to watch Paris 2024 Olympics as it's cut from BBC | TV & Radio | Showbiz & TV | Express.co.uk
author: Jake Hackney
description: BBC no longer holds the exclusive broadcasting rights for the Olympic Games. Luckily, a new deal means viewers can stream the entire schedule all in one place
